messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following online gaming platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"GameZone collects your username, email, date of birth, IP address, game activity, and in-game purchase history to operate the platform. We verify age to ensure compliance with child protection rules. Data is used for account management, fraud prevention, and personalised game recommendations. We share data with payment processors and anti-cheat service providers. Servers are in Frankfurt, Germany. Data is retained for 5 years after account closure."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated for any processing activity. Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) is implied for account operation — none are named.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Not applicable. Data collected does not constitute special category data.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Account management, fraud prevention, age verification, and personalised recommendations are each stated as distinct purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] IP address retention for 5 years post-account closure may be disproportionate for fraud prevention purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] 5 years after account closure is stated. Retention periods for specific data types (IP logs, payment records) are not differentiated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No data subject rights mentioned.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Payment processors and anti-cheat service providers referenced as categories but not named individually.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Frankfurt servers confirm EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 5 (IP address retention not justified, differentiated retention absent), Art. 15-21 (no rights), Art. 28 (processors not named), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing purpose
- Justify 5-year IP log retention or differentiate retention by data type
- Add full data subject rights section
- Name payment and anti-cheat processors
- Assess and appoint DPO if required
- Add breach notification commitment
